auth.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink auth` with one of: confirm, login, logout, profiles, status, test.

confirm.help: Clear the legacy pending-confirmation flag from older Bricklink builds. Current browser commands do not store confirmation codes locally. If BrickLink redirects to ``confirmation_code_required``, the command fails immediately and tells the user to check email and retry.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For browser sessions, BrickLink opens the LEGO account login; use the LEGO.com account email address and password, not the legacy BrickLink username. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth login`. For browser-session login, enter the LEGO.com account email address and password.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink auth profiles` with one of: create, delete, get, list, set-default.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth profiles create`. with required arguments name.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth profiles delete`. with required arguments name.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth profiles get`. with required arguments name. Use options such as --table as needed.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth profiles list`. Use options such as --table, --limit, --filter, --properties as needed.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).
status.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth status`. Use options such as --table as needed.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink auth test`. Use options such as --table as needed.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink cache` with one of: clear.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

catalog.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink catalog` with one of: colors, get, list, minifig, part, price, set, subsets, supersets.

colors.help: Get known colors for an item.
colors.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog colors`. with required arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog colors PART 3001`.

get.help: Get catalog item info by type and item number.
get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog get`. with required arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog get PART 3001`.

list.help: List catalog items by type using store inventory. The Bricklink API does not provide a catalog search endpoint. This command lists catalog items found in your store inventory, filtered by item type. Use 'catalog get' for direct lookups.
list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog list`. with required arguments item_type. Use options such as --table, --limit, --filter, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog list PART`.

minifig.help: Shortcut to get catalog info for a MINIFIG.
minifig.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog minifig`. with required arguments fig_no. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog minifig sw0001`.

part.help: Shortcut to get catalog info for a PART.
part.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog part`. with required arguments part_no. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog part 3001`.

price.help: Get price guide for an item. Supports bulk via --input or --stdin. Single item: bricklink catalog price PART 3001 bricklink catalog price PART 3001 --color 11 --condition N bricklink catalog price SET 75192-1 --sold Bulk (JSON file with list of {type, item_no, color?, condition?}): bricklink catalog price --input items.json cat items.json | bricklink catalog price --stdin
price.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog price`. and optional arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --color, --condition, --table as needed.

options.name: --sold
options.help: Show sold prices instead of stock

set.help: Shortcut to get catalog info for a SET.
set.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog set`. with required arguments set_no. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog set 75192-1`.

subsets.help: Get items contained in a set or item (subsets).
subsets.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog subsets`. with required arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog subsets SET 75192-1`.

supersets.help: Get sets or items that contain this item (supersets).
supersets.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink catalog supersets`. with required arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --color, --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink catalog supersets PART 3001`.

coupon.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink coupon` with one of: create, delete, get, list.

create.help: Create a coupon for a buyer.
examples: bricklink coupon create some_user --rate 3.00 --expires 2025-12-31 --remarks "Apology for missing part"
create.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink coupon create`. with required arguments buyer. Use options such as --type as needed. Example: `bricklink coupon create some_user --rate 5.00`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink coupon delete`. with required arguments coupon_id. Example: `bricklink coupon delete 12345`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink coupon get`. with required arguments coupon_id. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink coupon get 12345`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink coupon list`. Use options such as --direction, --status, --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink coupon list`.

inventory.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink inventory` with one of: create, delete, get, list, search, stats, stockroom, update, update-qty.

create.help: Create inventory item(s). Single item: provide type, item_no, and options. Bulk: provide --input or --stdin with JSON array.
create.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory create`. and optional arguments type, item_no. Use options such as --color, --condition as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory create PART 3001 --color 11 --quantity 10 --price 0.05 --condition N`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory delete`. with required arguments inventory_id. Example: `bricklink inventory delete 123456`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory get`. with required arguments inventory_id. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory get 123456`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory list`. Use options such as --type, --status, --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory list`.

search.help: Search inventory by part number. Fetches all inventories and filters client-side by item number.
search.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory search`. with required arguments part_no. Use options such as --color, --type, --condition, --table as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory search 3001`.

options.name: --prefix
options.help: Match items starting with part_no (e.g., 2495 matches 2495c01)

stats.help: Get inventory statistics. Computes totals for lots, items, and value across all available inventory, broken down by item type.
stats.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory stats`. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory stats`.

stockroom.help: List stockroom items. Shows items in stockroom A, B, C, or all stockrooms.
stockroom.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory stockroom`. Use options such as --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink inventory stockroom`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory update`. with required arguments inventory_id. Example: `bricklink inventory update 123456 --price 0.15`.

options.name: --stockroom
options.help: Move to stockroom (A, B, C, or 'none' to remove)

update-qty.help: Bulk update inventory quantities. Expects input as JSON array of objects with inventory_id and quantity fields. By default, quantity is treated as a relative change (+/-). Use --set-qty for absolute.
update-qty.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink inventory update-qty`. Example: `bricklink inventory update-qty --input items.json`.

member.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink member` with one of: delete-note, note, ratings, set-note.

delete-note.help: Delete personal note for a member.
delete-note.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink member delete-note`. with required arguments username. Example: `bricklink member delete-note some_user`.

note.help: Get personal note for a member.
note.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink member note`. with required arguments username. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink member note some_user`.

ratings.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink member ratings`. with required arguments username. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink member ratings some_user`.

set-note.help: Create or update a personal note for a member.
examples: bricklink member set-note some_user "Good buyer, fast payment"
bricklink member set-note some_user "Problem buyer - opened dispute"
set-note.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink member set-note`. with required arguments username, text. Example: `bricklink member set-note some_user "Good buyer, fast payment"`.

messages.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink messages` with one of: get, list, mark-read, mark-unread, reply, send.

get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages get`. with required arguments message_id. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink messages get 12345678`.

options.name: --max-search-messages
options.help: Max messages to scan when building history (default 50)

list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages list`. Use options such as --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink messages list`.

options.name: --order-id
options.help: Get messages for a specific order (API-based, no browser needed)

options.name: --include-history
options.help: Include message history thread for each message

options.name: --max-search-messages
options.help: Max messages to scan when building history (default 50)

mark-read.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages mark-read`. with required arguments message_id. Example: `bricklink messages mark-read 12345678`.

mark-unread.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages mark-unread`. with required arguments message_id. Example: `bricklink messages mark-unread 12345678`.

examples: bricklink messages reply 12345678 "Thank you for your message!"
bricklink messages reply 12345678 "Here is the tracking info" --copy
reply.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages reply`. with required arguments message_id, body. Example: `bricklink messages reply 12345678 "Thank you for your message!"`.

send.help: Send a message to a member or linked to an order (browser-based). When sending to an order:  bricklink messages send ORDER_ID BODY When sending to a member:  bricklink messages send --member USERNAME BODY
examples: bricklink messages send 12345678 "Your order has been shipped!"
bricklink messages send --member sydneybrickemp "Hello!" --subject "Re: Your message"
send.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink messages send`. and optional arguments arg1, arg2. Example: `bricklink messages send 12345678 "Your order has been shipped!"`.

arguments.name: arg1
arguments.help: [ARG1]  Order ID (when sending to an order) or message body (when using --member)

arguments.name: arg2
arguments.help: [ARG2]  Message body (when order ID is provided as first argument)

options.name: --member
options.help: Bricklink username to send message to (uses contact form)

notification.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink notification` with one of: get, list, send-wanted-list.

get.help: Get details about a notification type.
get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink notification get`. and optional arguments notification_type. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink notification get wanted_list`.

list.help: List available notification actions. Shows the notification types that can be sent via the browser.
list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink notification list`. Use options such as --table, --limit, --filter, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink notification list`.

send-wanted-list.help: Send wanted list notification to all matching stores.
send-wanted-list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink notification send-wanted-list`. Example: `bricklink notification send-wanted-list`.

order.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink order` with one of: file, get, items, list, search, ship, update-status.

file.help: File an order (set is_filed to true).
file.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order file`. with required arguments order_id. Example: `bricklink order file 12345678`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order get`. with required arguments order_id. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink order get 12345678`.

items.help: Get items in an order. The API returns items in nested batch lists. This command flattens them into a single list.
items.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order items`. with required arguments order_id. Use options such as --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink order items 12345678`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order list`. Use options such as --status, --direction, --table, --filter, --limit, --properties as needed. Example: `bricklink order list`.

search.help: Search orders by item number (browser-based). Finds all orders containing the specified item. Uses browser automation since this feature is not available via the API.
search.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order search`. with required arguments item_no. Use options such as --type, --color, --condition, --status, --direction, --table as needed. Example: `bricklink order search 3001`.

ship.help: Mark order as shipped, optionally with tracking info. Updates shipping details first (if provided), then sets status to SHIPPED.
ship.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order ship`. with required arguments order_id. Use options such as --tracking, --link as needed. Example: `bricklink order ship 12345678`.

update-status.help: Update order status. Valid statuses: PENDING, UPDATED, PROCESSING, READY, PAID, PACKED, SHIPPED, RECEIVED, COMPLETED, OCR, NPB, NPX, NRS, NSS, CANCELLED
update-status.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink order update-status`. with required arguments order_id, status. Example: `bricklink order update-status 12345678 PACKED`.

refund.usage_instructions: Use `bricklink refund` with one of: full, info, issue.

full.help: Issue a full refund for an order (browser-based). Default reason: "Buyer and Seller agreed to cancel order"
examples: bricklink refund full 12345678 --reason "Seller cannot complete transaction"
bricklink refund full 12345678 --details "Out of stock on multiple items"
full.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink refund full`. with required arguments order_id. Example: `bricklink refund full 12345678`.

info.help: Get refund page info for an order (browser-based). Shows transaction ID, payment processor, prior refunds, buyer info, and refund activity history.
info.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink refund info`. with required arguments order_id. Use options such as --table as needed. Example: `bricklink refund info 12345678`.

issue.help: Issue a partial refund for an order (browser-based). Default reason: "Item was missing or unsatisfactory"
issue.usage_instructions: Run `bricklink refund issue`. with required arguments order_id, amount. Example: `bricklink refund issue 12345678 5.00`.

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Fill form and verify Review is enabled; do NOT submit

usage_instructions: Use `bricklink <command>` to access Bricklink API and browser workflows. Top-level command groups: auth, cache, catalog, coupon, inventory, member, messages, notification, order, refund. Run `bricklink <command> --help` for command-specific arguments and options.
